step_by_step_reasoning: The transcript outlines two primary flows: a real-time recommendation request and an offline training/ingestion loop. 1) The real-time loop starts at the Peloton Bike (UserConsumerIOT), passing to Hydration Service, then Recommendation Service. Recommendation Service retrieves user data from DynamoDB, candidates from Neptune (Graph DB), and models from S3 (Model Registry). It calls Inference Service to score the candidates, registers events with MSK, and sends recommendations back to the user. 2) The offline loop uses MWAA to orchestrate EMR (for pre-processing, updating DynamoDB and Neptune, and staging historical S3 data) and EKS GPU clusters to train machine learning models and register artifacts to S3.

graph.name: Peloton Personalized Recommendation System
graph.notes: Powers recommendations for over 6.5M members. Combines real-time microservices on EKS with an offline EMR and EKS GPU-based training pipeline orchestrated by MWAA.

nodes.name: Hydration Service
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Backend microservice that initiates recommendation orchestration.

nodes.name: Feature Store
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Low-latency store holding user preferences and historical behavior.

nodes.name: Graph Database
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores relational graph of members, classes, and taken rides.

nodes.name: Inference Service
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Scores candidate workouts using machine learning models.

nodes.name: Event Logging Stream
nodes.notes: Streams real-time metadata and telemetry back to S3.

nodes.name: Data Lake & Model Registry
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Houses historical datasets, logged events, and model artifacts.

nodes.name: EMR Spark Clusters
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Performs offline batch preprocessing and low-latency store sync.

nodes.name: MWAA (Airflow)
nodes.notes: Orchestrates batch pre-processing and model training pipelines.

edges.notes: Pushes candidates, features, and model for scoring.
Persists streamed data for future training loop.
Hydrates Feature Store with batch metrics.
Hydrates Graph Database with updated relationships.
